title: Relationships Between Nilpotency and Curvature
speaker: Aaron Naber
abstract: We travel the years in order to understand the relationship between Nilpotency and Riemannian geometry: including Gromov's almost flat theorem for manifolds with bounded curvature and Fukaya-Yamaguchi's almost nilpotency of spaces with lower sectional bounds.  Time allowing we will discuss the Milnor conjecture and nilpotency on spaces with lower Ricci bounds.   Required background should be minimal and most of our discussions should take place in the form of concrete examples.

proof_stages.label: Curvature and nilpotency vocabulary
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets the guiding theme that curvature degeneration is governed by nilpotent algebra, then recalls Riemann and Ricci curvature and nilpotent groups via the lower central series.

proof_stages.label: Almost-flat manifolds and nilmanifold topology
proof_stages.summary: Passing from zero curvature to small curvature leads to collapsed nilmanifold geometries, culminating in Gromov’s almost-flat theorem that diameter-normalized compact manifolds with sufficiently small curvature are virtually infranil/nilmanifold quotients.

proof_stages.label: Fukaya’s local bounded-curvature classification
proof_stages.summary: Gromov’s global almost-flat theorem is localized: a bounded-curvature ball has a definite open neighborhood diffeomorphic to a vector bundle over an infranilmanifold, giving the local topology of bounded-curvature spaces.

proof_stages.label: Lower sectional curvature and residual algebraic topology
proof_stages.summary: After weakening from two-sided curvature bounds to lower curvature bounds, full topological control is lost but Gromov’s lower-sectional theorem still bounds cohomology, and the Bott ellipticity conjecture predicts stronger rational homotopy restrictions for nonnegative sectional curvature.

proof_stages.label: Nilpotent fundamental groups under Ricci and sectional bounds
proof_stages.summary: Milnor’s nonnegative-Ricci theorem, Gromov’s polynomial-growth theorem, and Fukaya–Yamaguchi’s local lower-sectional result show that fundamental groups or local fundamental groups under lower curvature bounds are forced to be virtually nilpotent, with an almost-abelian conjecture in the nonnegative-sectional case.

proof_stages.label: Kapovitch–Wilking refinement and controlled non-finite generation
proof_stages.summary: Using Fukaya–Yamaguchi style local arguments together with Ricci-limit structure theory, finitely generated subgroups of nonnegative-Ricci fundamental groups have uniformly bounded generators, so non-finitely generated examples can occur only through controlled ascending behavior such as subgroups of the rationals in the circle.

proof_stages.label: Dyadic universal-cover tree construction
proof_stages.summary: To realize dyadic/rational fundamental groups, the construction builds a simply connected space with a proper free group action by arranging S^3×R^4 pieces in an infinite tree whose successive levels identify larger finite subgroups.

proof_stages.label: Positive-Ricci mapping-class gluing
proof_stages.summary: The key Ricci-geometric ingredient is that on S^3×S^3 the standard metric and its pullback by mapping-class-nontrivial diffeomorphisms can be connected through positive-Ricci metrics, enabling the nontrivial gluings; the discussion clarifies that this relies on Ricci curvature’s averaged nature and high-dimensional squashing rather than sectional control.
